verses.text: I declare the gospel 'wherein ye stand; by which also ye are saved.' The saving word. The gospel is the ground of our salvation.
I declare the gospel 'wherein ye stand; by which also ye are saved.' The saving word. The gospel is the ground of our salvation.
'Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures... and... rose again the third day.' The core. Death and resurrection are the heart of the gospel.
'Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures... and... rose again the third day.' The core. Death and resurrection are the heart of the gospel.
He was seen of Cephas, the twelve, 'above five hundred,' James, and last of Paul. Many witnesses. The resurrection is well attested.
He was seen of Cephas, the twelve, 'above five hundred,' James, and last of Paul. Many witnesses. The resurrection is well attested.
He was seen of Cephas, the twelve, 'above five hundred,' James, and last of Paul. Many witnesses. The resurrection is well attested.
He was seen of Cephas, the twelve, 'above five hundred,' James, and last of Paul. Many witnesses. The resurrection is well attested.
I am 'the least of the apostles'; 'by the grace of God I am what I am.' Humble grace. He owes all to grace, not merit.
I am 'the least of the apostles'; 'by the grace of God I am what I am.' Humble grace. He owes all to grace, not merit.
I am 'the least of the apostles'; 'by the grace of God I am what I am.' Humble grace. He owes all to grace, not merit.
If there be no resurrection, Christ is not risen, and 'your faith is also vain.' At stake. The whole gospel hangs on the rising of Christ.
If there be no resurrection, Christ is not risen, and 'your faith is also vain.' At stake. The whole gospel hangs on the rising of Christ.
If there be no resurrection, Christ is not risen, and 'your faith is also vain.' At stake. The whole gospel hangs on the rising of Christ.
If there be no resurrection, Christ is not risen, and 'your faith is also vain.' At stake. The whole gospel hangs on the rising of Christ.
If Christ be not raised, ye are 'yet in your sins'; we are 'of all men most miserable.' No hope without it. Without the resurrection, faith is empty.
If Christ be not raised, ye are 'yet in your sins'; we are 'of all men most miserable.' No hope without it. Without the resurrection, faith is empty.
If Christ be not raised, ye are 'yet in your sins'; we are 'of all men most miserable.' No hope without it. Without the resurrection, faith is empty.
If Christ be not raised, ye are 'yet in your sins'; we are 'of all men most miserable.' No hope without it. Without the resurrection, faith is empty.
'But now is Christ risen from the dead'; 'as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.' The firstfruits. His rising secures ours.
'But now is Christ risen from the dead'; 'as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.' The firstfruits. His rising secures ours.
'But now is Christ risen from the dead'; 'as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.' The firstfruits. His rising secures ours.
Every man in his order; 'the last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.' Death conquered. Christ shall abolish death itself.
Every man in his order; 'the last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.' Death conquered. Christ shall abolish death itself.
Every man in his order; 'the last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.' Death conquered. Christ shall abolish death itself.
Every man in his order; 'the last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.' Death conquered. Christ shall abolish death itself.
All shall be subdued under Him, 'that God may be all in all.' Final glory. The end is God supreme over all.
All shall be subdued under Him, 'that God may be all in all.' Final glory. The end is God supreme over all.
Why stand we in jeopardy, if the dead rise not? 'let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.' Vain living. Without hope of rising, life is mere indulgence.
Why stand we in jeopardy, if the dead rise not? 'let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.' Vain living. Without hope of rising, life is mere indulgence.
Why stand we in jeopardy, if the dead rise not? 'let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.' Vain living. Without hope of rising, life is mere indulgence.
Why stand we in jeopardy, if the dead rise not? 'let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.' Vain living. Without hope of rising, life is mere indulgence.
'Evil communications corrupt good manners'; awake to righteousness, and sin not. A warning. Bad company and error corrupt the soul.
'Evil communications corrupt good manners'; awake to righteousness, and sin not. A warning. Bad company and error corrupt the soul.
How are the dead raised? thou sowest 'bare grain'; God gives it a body. Like a seed. Burial is sowing; resurrection is the harvest.
How are the dead raised? thou sowest 'bare grain'; God gives it a body. Like a seed. Burial is sowing; resurrection is the harvest.
How are the dead raised? thou sowest 'bare grain'; God gives it a body. Like a seed. Burial is sowing; resurrection is the harvest.
How are the dead raised? thou sowest 'bare grain'; God gives it a body. Like a seed. Burial is sowing; resurrection is the harvest.
There are bodies celestial and terrestrial; 'one star differeth from another star in glory.' Varied glory. The risen bodies will differ in splendour.
There are bodies celestial and terrestrial; 'one star differeth from another star in glory.' Varied glory. The risen bodies will differ in splendour.
There are bodies celestial and terrestrial; 'one star differeth from another star in glory.' Varied glory. The risen bodies will differ in splendour.
Sown in corruption, raised in incorruption; 'it is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body.' Transformed. The grave gives back a glorious body.
Sown in corruption, raised in incorruption; 'it is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body.' Transformed. The grave gives back a glorious body.
Sown in corruption, raised in incorruption; 'it is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body.' Transformed. The grave gives back a glorious body.
The last Adam 'a quickening spirit'; 'we shall... bear the image of the heavenly.' Christ's likeness. The risen saints shall be like their Lord.
The last Adam 'a quickening spirit'; 'we shall... bear the image of the heavenly.' Christ's likeness. The risen saints shall be like their Lord.
The last Adam 'a quickening spirit'; 'we shall... bear the image of the heavenly.' Christ's likeness. The risen saints shall be like their Lord.
The last Adam 'a quickening spirit'; 'we shall... bear the image of the heavenly.' Christ's likeness. The risen saints shall be like their Lord.
The last Adam 'a quickening spirit'; 'we shall... bear the image of the heavenly.' Christ's likeness. The risen saints shall be like their Lord.
Flesh cannot inherit the kingdom; 'we shall all be changed, in a moment... at the last trump.' Sudden change. The living and dead are transformed at His coming.
Flesh cannot inherit the kingdom; 'we shall all be changed, in a moment... at the last trump.' Sudden change. The living and dead are transformed at His coming.
Flesh cannot inherit the kingdom; 'we shall all be changed, in a moment... at the last trump.' Sudden change. The living and dead are transformed at His coming.
Flesh cannot inherit the kingdom; 'we shall all be changed, in a moment... at the last trump.' Sudden change. The living and dead are transformed at His coming.
'Death is swallowed up in victory. O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?' Triumph. Death is conquered for ever in Christ.
'Death is swallowed up in victory. O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?' Triumph. Death is conquered for ever in Christ.
The sting of death is sin; 'thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory'; 'your labour is not in vain.' Sure victory. Christ gives the triumph, so work on with hope.
The sting of death is sin; 'thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory'; 'your labour is not in vain.' Sure victory. Christ gives the triumph, so work on with hope.
